title: Owner Directive Compliance System (governance/control)
objective: Implement a durable Owner Directive Compliance System (directive D-001): registry, shared resolver, project_control.py enforcement, skill + skill edits, verifier agent, validator + CI, reminder hook, adversarial harnesses. Lead-only; open one PR; do not merge/accept.
business_reason: Eliminate reliance on chat memory: every substantive owner directive becomes durable, atomic, traceable, independently verified repository state before completion can be declared (D-001).
directive_regime_note: M0-T023 was created under the pre-enforcement (old) CLI as the bootstrap task and hand-stamped in-regime (directive_regime_version 1.0) with directive_refs to D-001. From this point the hardened CLI enforces refs/evidence/verification on its transitions. See project-control/directives/BOOTSTRAP.md.
forbidden_paths: project-control/tasks/ (any task file other than M0-T023.json)
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1: python tools/validate_directive_compliance.py --check exits 0 over the D-001 registry (schemas, unique IDs, source hashes, append-only IDs, producer/verifier separation).
AS-2: claiming an in-regime task (directive_regime_version set) with no valid directive_refs is refused; with D-001 ALL refs it succeeds.
AS-3: a task citing only a strict subset of D-001's applicable requirements is refused for selective citation.
AS-4: every currently accepted task remains byte-immutable; each in-flight task status (claimed/awaiting_gate/blocked/backlog) has a proven legal path forward (migration table) with no deadlock.

AS-5: the reminder hook emits a bounded advisory (IDs + summaries + pointers, under the size cap) on SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit, never the full protocol; a corrupt registry yields a visible fail-closed warning, not silence.
AS-6: readonly_agent_guard denies mutating Bash/write tools for directive-compliance-verifier (added to the roster) and its regression test asserts it.
AS-7: frozen-evidence identity uses a path-scoped content manifest: it stays stable across merge/rebase/squash of identical content and goes stale on any relevant-file content change.
AS-8: no new dependency; all new tools import only the Python standard library; agent_dispatch_guard.py/readonly_agent_guard.py existing tests still pass.
AS-9: the independent directive-compliance-verifier reports every D-001 requirement ID individually (SATISFIED/VIOLATED/INDETERMINATE) at the frozen head; producer != verifier.

gate_role_map.G4: independent directive-compliance final verification (directive-compliance-verifier) + control-plane integrity (control-plane-verifier)

gate_notes: G1 (data-contract) is NOT_APPLICABLE: this governance task adds no official-source connector or external data mapping. This is recorded as an explicit, justified N/A, not an omission.
risks: Enforcement mis-scope could deadlock in-flight work; mitigated by the effective-date + regime-entry migration rule and the no-deadlock migration table (D-001-R108/R109).
Content-manifest identity must survive merge/rebase/squash yet invalidate on relevant edits; covered by adversarial tests (D-001-R110/R111).
